name: helix-personmatching

metadata.name: helix.personmatching
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# helix.personmatching

## Entrypoint [helix_personmatching/matchers/matcher.py](helix_personmatching/matchers/matcher.py) ## Inputs This code takes in: 1. A source Patient/Person resource or a Bundle of Patient/Person resources 2. A target Patient/Person resource or a Bundle of Patient/Person resources 3. A set of rule options/weights 4. Whether to return only successful matches or all match results 5. (Optional) Matching threshold (between 0 and 1). Can also be set as an environment variable: `PERSON_MATCH_THRESHOLD` 6. (Optional) Average score boost (between 0 and 1). Can also be set as an environment variable: `PERSON_MATCH_AVERAGE_SCORE_BOOST` ## Outputs 1. A list of match results where id_source is id of source record and id_target is id of matched record. # Logic 1. The code runs through each rule and calculates a uniqueness probability (between 0 and 1) if the rule matches. 2. The code then picks the rule with the highest uniqueness probability and uses that probability 3. The code then calculates the average of all the other rule probabilities and boosts the uniqueness probability by it. 4. The code then sees if any other boosting rules were requested and boosts the probability by those # Weights There are five cases when matching fields of two records in a rule and the weights that are applied for each case: 1. Field present in both sides and match -> `exact_match` 2. Field present in both sides and partial match -> `partial_match` 3. Field missing in both sides -> `missing` 4. Field missing in one side -> `missing` 5. Field present in both sides but do not match -> 0.0 For boosting rules, there is an additional `boost` weight.

documentation:
Calculate a matching score for one rule between FHIR Person-Person, or Person-Patient, or Person/Patient-AppUser

```

param source

param target

Dictionary of Pii data for FHIR Person/Patient data, or AppUser data

param verbose

set to True to enable logging

return

Dictionary of 1 rule score result
```

documentation: required, "usual | official | temp | nickname | anonymous | old | maiden"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.TELECOM_SYSTEM
documentation: required, "phone | fax | email | pager | url | sms | other"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.TELECOM_USE
documentation: required, "home | work | temp | old | mobile"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.ADDRESS_USE
documentation: required, "home | work | temp | old | billing"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.ADDRESS_TYPE
documentation: required, "postal | physical | both"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.LINK_ASSURANCE
documentation: required, "level1 | level2 | level3 | level4"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.EMAIL
documentation: WHEN telecom.system = "email"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.EMAIL_USERNAME
documentation: email username, AttributeEntrying before the "@"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.PHONE
documentation: WHEN telecom.system = "phone"

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.PHONE_AREA
documentation: phone area number (first 3 digit)

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.PHONE_LOCAL
documentation: phone local number (middle 3 digit)

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.PHONE_LINE
documentation: phone line number (last 4 digit)

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.BIRTH_DATE_YEAR
documentation: YYYY from birthDate

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.BIRTH_DATE_MONTH
documentation: MM from birthDate

class_variables.name: helix_personmatching.models.constants.Attribute.BIRTH_DATE_DAY
documentation: DD from birthDate

variables.name: helix_personmatching.matchers.matcher.regex
documentation: allow _ since some resources in our fhir server have that

methods.name: helix_personmatching.matchers.matcher.Matcher.__init__
documentation:
the standard probability for a rule is 0.95. The high uniqueness rules are higher.
the boost for average score is at max of 0.01
So we want the threshold to be higher than 0.95 so if the average of the rules in 0.9 then it passes
0.095 + (0.9 * 0.001) = 0.0959
or if the special boost of 0.5 is passed then a score of 0.90 + 0.05 will pass

```
param threshold

all matches below this threshold are rejected

param average_score_boost

We multiply the average of all rule scores by this and add to the
highest probability rule
```

methods.name: helix_personmatching.mergers.practitioner_merger.PractitionerMerger.merge
documentation:
Merge practitioner resources from multiple data sets into a single practitioner resource.
Takes in a dictionary with the following fields as arrays:
practitioner
practitionerrole
organization
location
insuranceplan
endpoint
schedule
group
measurereport

```
param row

the row to merge

param config

the merge config

param graph

the graph
```
